commands.observation: The `fine_tuning_contract` test binary finished successfully with 1/1 tests passing: `job_lifecycle_checkpoint_listing_and_permission_admin_semantics`.
The `fine_tuning_controls_contract` test binary finished successfully with 1/1 tests passing: `method_variants_and_job_controls_remain_distinct`.
The `graders_contract` test binary finished successfully with 1/1 tests passing: `graders_validate_configs_and_run_tiny_samples`.

assertions.reason: `fine_tuning_contract` proves mocked create/retrieve/list/cancel behavior for a fine-tuning job, and `fine_tuning_controls_contract` separately proves `jobs.list_events(...)` returns typed `fine_tuning.job.event` records. However, the observed mocked fixtures do not show an event record for the same cancelled job or tie the listed event output to the cancel transition, so the full lifecycle-plus-matching-event clause is not completely proven on this surface.

The mocked checkpoint listing coverage proves the `/fine_tuning/jobs/{id}/checkpoints` route, checkpoint `id`, `fine_tuned_model_checkpoint`, `metrics.train_loss`, and unknown metric preservation via `metrics.extra["custom_metric"]`. But the test never asserts `step_number`, and source inspection shows `FineTuningCheckpoint.step_number` is `#[serde(default)]`, so the currently observed pass does not fully prove the checkpoint identity envelope required by the contract.

The same mocked test proves checkpoint permission create/list/delete route shapes, preserves `checkpoint.permission` objects with `project_id`, and surfaces a typed `ErrorKind::Api(ApiErrorKind::PermissionDenied)` with API code `permission_denied` when the permission-create path is exercised with an ordinary project key.

`graders_contract` validates both `score_model` and `label_model` grader definitions and proves `run(...)` returns structured `reward`, `sub_rewards`, per-model token-usage metadata, grader type/name metadata, and explicit structured error flags for tiny mocked samples.

The mocked controls test proves distinct `supervised` and `reinforcement` create payloads plus typed `list_events`, `pause`, and `resume` operations. However, the contract explicitly names `dpo` alongside those methods, and no assigned mocked cargo test exercises a DPO create payload even though the SDK surface exposes that variant, so the full method-variant matrix remains unproven.

frictions.description: Cargo output only reports passing test function names; mapping those passes back to assertion-level behavior required reading the assigned fine-tuning contract tests and, for defaulted fields, the corresponding resource model definitions.
frictions.resolution: Reviewed `tests/fine_tuning_contract.rs`, `tests/fine_tuning_controls_contract.rs`, `tests/graders_contract.rs`, and the relevant `src/resources/fine_tuning.rs` model defaults before assigning assertion statuses.

blockers.description: No observed mocked fixture ties `jobs.cancel(...)` to a corresponding `jobs.list_events(...)` record for the same fine-tuning job, so the event portion of VAL-ADV-001 is only partially covered.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Inspected both assigned fine-tuning test binaries; one covers create/retrieve/list/cancel and the other covers event listing, but no fixture combines cancel with event retrieval for the same job.

blockers.description: The observed checkpoint-listing test does not assert `step_number`, and the SDK model defaults that field during deserialization, so VAL-ADV-002 cannot be fully proven from the assigned mocked cargo surface.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Inspected `tests/fine_tuning_contract.rs` and confirmed it asserts checkpoint identity and metrics but not `step_number`; then verified in `src/resources/fine_tuning.rs` that `FineTuningCheckpoint.step_number` uses `#[serde(default)]`.

blockers.description: The assigned mocked controls coverage does not exercise the DPO fine-tuning method variant named by VAL-ADV-013.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Searched the assigned fine-tuning tests and found coverage for `FineTuningMethod::Supervised` and `FineTuningMethod::Reinforcement` only; no assigned mocked test invokes `FineTuningMethod::Dpo`.

summary: Ran all 3 assigned mocked cargo commands successfully. Confirmed VAL-ADV-003 and VAL-ADV-004, and marked VAL-ADV-001, VAL-ADV-002, and VAL-ADV-013 blocked because the assigned mocked cargo surface leaves specific contract clauses unproven.
